assets.name: lecture_08_figure_01.png
assets.rationale: All four candidates are Stanford University intro title cards without blackboard mathematics, diagrams, or lecture-layout evidence. They are visually clean but not useful for note writing, so this subtitle window should be skipped.

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the clearest and most useful of the four. Both bracket equations are fully legible, Susskind has stopped actively blocking the lower line with his hand, and the sparse board layout is preserved cleanly. It captures the mathematical point of the moment without the extra occlusion present in the earlier pointing frames.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Hamiltonian doesn't change says there's a symmetry.
assets.caption_hint: Poisson brackets vanishing with the Hamiltonian

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest and most complete view of the board diagram. The geometric sketch on the right board is fully visible, Susskind has moved his hand away so the line and nested closed curves are not being actively occluded, and the board layout is cleaner than in the earlier pointing frames. The partial equations at the top are still fragmentary, so the real value here is the diagram evidence.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The other contribution is going to be the potential energy.

assets.name: lecture_08_figure_04.png
assets.subtitle_excerpt: let's just calculate the potential energy

assets.subtitle_excerpt: The higher it is the more potential energy
